ServiceNow Business Analyst

Location: 100% Remote (USA)

Employment Type: W2 or 1099

Rate: Up to $45/hour (Non-Negotiable)

Visa Requirement: U.S. Citizen or Green Card Holders Only

Job Summary

We are seeking a mid-level ServiceNow Business Analyst to join our client's team on a 12-month remote engagement. The ideal candidate will have experience gathering business requirements, documenting functional specifications, and collaborating with stakeholders to deliver successful ServiceNow implementations. This role requires strong communication skills, analytical thinking, and a solid understanding of ServiceNow platform capabilities.

Candidates with ServiceNow certifications are strongly preferred.

Required Qualifications

3–6 years of experience as a Business Analyst, with at least 2+ years working on ServiceNow projects

Experience gathering, analyzing, and documenting business and functional requirements

Strong understanding of ServiceNow platform capabilities and best practices

Experience facilitating workshops, stakeholder meetings, and requirement discovery sessions

Excellent communication and documentation skills

Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments

Ability to work independently in a fully remote environment

Preferred Qualifications

ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA)

ServiceNow Certified Implementation Specialist (CIS) or other ServiceNow certifications

Experience supporting one or more ServiceNow modules such as:

ITSM

HRSD

CSM

SPM

Knowledge of ServiceNow workflows, forms, service catalogs, and reporting

Key Responsibilities

Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document business requirements.

Conduct requirement workshops, interviews, and process mapping sessions.

Translate business needs into detailed functional requirements and user stories.

Collaborate with ServiceNow developers, architects, and QA teams throughout the project lifecycle.

Support solution design by ensuring business requirements align with ServiceNow best practices.

Assist with backlog management, sprint planning, and prioritization activities.

Review configurations and enhancements to ensure they meet business expectations.

Coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT), capture feedback, and support issue resolution.

Create process documentation, business workflows, and functional specifications.

Provide ongoing support during implementation, deployment, and post-production activities.

If interested, email your resumes at aarti@technodeed.com

Immediate Job opening for "Veeva Safety Analyst"

Role: Veeva Safety Analyst

Location: Cambridge, MA

Role Descriptions: The Veeva Safety Functional & Solution Analyst is responsible for translating business requirements into effective Veeva Safety solutions while ensuring alignment with platform capabilities and organizational needs. This role plays a key part in delivering high-quality solutions, minimizing rework, and ensuring seamless collaboration between business and technical teams.

Key Responsibilities:

· Veeva Safety Expertise: Leverage deep experience in Veeva Safety, including configuration and platform capabilities, to design efficient and scalable solutions.

· Requirements Translation & Solution Design: Convert business requirements into detailed functional and solution specifications within Veeva Safety. Perform impact analysis on existing configurations and system dependencies to ensure solution feasibility and integrity.

· Collaboration & Delivery Support: Work closely with cross-functional teams including Project Managers, Business Analysts, Vault Developers, Testers, and business stakeholders (e.g., Biogen team). Act as a bridge between business and technical teams to ensure clarity and alignment.

· Quality Assurance & Optimization: Contribute to high-quality IT delivery by minimizing gaps between requirements and development outputs. Reduce defects, rework, and multiple testing cycles by ensuring accurate and complete solution definition.

· Stakeholder Communication: Demonstrate strong communication skills, including active listening and the ability

to clearly articulate solution options, trade-offs, and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
